statements.sql: INSERT INTO silver.IfElseTarget (ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, PriceCategory) SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)), ProductName, 'Premium' FROM bronze.Product WHERE ListPrice > 100
INSERT INTO silver.IfElseTarget (ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, PriceCategory) SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)), ProductName, 'Standard' FROM bronze.Product

refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT CAST(src.ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, src.ProductName AS EnglishProductName, CAST(NULL AS NVARCHAR(20)) AS PriceCategory FROM [bronze].[Product] AS src
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH import AS ( SELECT CAST(src.ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, src.ProductName AS EnglishProductName, CAST(NULL AS NVARCHAR(20)) AS PriceCategory FROM [bronze].[Product] AS src ), logical AS ( SELECT i.ProductAlternateKey, i.EnglishProductName, i.PriceCategory, CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM silver.IfElseTarget) THEN 'update' ELSE 'insert' END AS operation_type FROM import i ), final AS ( SELECT ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, PriceCategory FROM logical ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both extracted and refactored SQL access the same source table: [bronze].[Product] AS src

output_columns.summary: Output columns match in name and type: ProductAlternateKey (NVARCHAR(25)), EnglishProductName (NVARCHAR(50)), PriceCategory (NVARCHAR(20)). Refactored query includes operation_type in intermediate logical CTE but excludes it from final SELECT, matching extracted output.

joins.summary: No joins in either query. Both queries read directly from bronze.Product. The refactored query adds an EXISTS subquery check against silver.IfElseTarget but this is a logical decision driver, not a join that affects source data selection.

filters.summary: Both queries have no WHERE clause filters. Extracted query selects all rows from bronze.Product. Refactored query preserves this behavior in the import CTE and final SELECT, with no filtering applied.

aggregation_grain.summary: Aggregation grain is consistent. Both queries operate at row level with no GROUP BY, aggregation functions, or row reduction. Refactored query maintains 1:1 row mapping from source through import → logical → final CTEs.
